metadata.purpose: Implementation-ready starter repair plan for Codex. Combines global repair directives with automated review flags from the extracted question bank.

global_repairs.issue: No debug/development/fallback notes in learner UI.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Confirm no learner-facing UI contains legacyCheckpoints, checkpointBank=legacy, active-dev, development testing, debug, or fallback instructions. Keep reminders only in docs/DEV_NOTES.md, reports, or source comments.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: Run audit:learner-copy and add failing patterns if missing. Remove any visible debug bank indicator from Home/Journey/Play/Glossary/Badge.

global_repairs.issue: Question bank needs implementation-ready review artifacts.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Generate JSON, CSV, and PDF review packets with full question bank, repairs applied, question type distribution, and open review concerns.

global_repairs.issue: Question type balance should support badge validity.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Maintain a mixture of mechanism, boundary, causal-consequence, applied scenario, human-use-judgment, misconception-check, and synthesis. Avoid too many same-type questions in a single card.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: Add a question-type distribution table by stage/card to the v0.27.13 report.

global_repairs.issue: Correct answer should not be obvious by length/style.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Run an answer-length bias audit. Review high-ratio items and balance answer choice length and specificity.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: Add audit warnings for correct answers >1.35× mean distractor length and >25 characters longer than average distractor.

global_repairs.recommended_change: Review early use of RAG, retrieval, softmax, logits, diffusion, grounding, hallucination, and context window. Either explain or replace future-stage terms before they are taught.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: Add or update review notes per flagged item; do not remove useful glossary-near distractors if feedback makes the boundary clear.

global_repairs.issue: Distractors should be tempting but fair.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Review distractors using absolute words such as must, every, always, guaranteed, definitely. Keep when they represent a real myth; otherwise make them less cartoonish.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: For obviously silly distractors, replace with a nearby glossary misconception and feedback that teaches the boundary.

global_repairs.issue: Set-aside validation examples must stay clear.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Keep learner-facing wording: “set-aside validation examples are kept out of training and saved for testing.” Avoid unexplained held-out/holdout wording.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: Search for held-out/holdout and replace or define; validate with audit:learner-copy or audit:checkpoints.

global_repairs.issue: Scroll behavior is part of assessment usability.
global_repairs.recommended_change: Preserve reliable scroll-to-checkpoint and scroll-to-top behavior when advancing questions/cards; include mobile Safari QA.
global_repairs.implementation_instruction: Do not regress v0.27.11 scroll fixes. Test 2-, 3-, 4-, and 5-question cards at 320px and 390px.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: An alignment-shaped assistant refuses a harmful request and follows a safer instruction instead. What should a model-literate learner conclude?
flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: Its behavior was shaped toward instructions and safety boundaries, but that does not prove moral understanding.
flagged_question_repairs.recommended_change: Shorten the correct answer or lengthen/tighten distractors so the correct choice is not obviously more complete. Review “definitely/guaranteed/must” distractors; keep only if they represent a real public myth, otherwise make them more plausible.
flagged_question_repairs.implementation_instruction: Codex should revise this question only if the issue is confirmed in source data; preserve stable IDs unless meaning changes, and create new IDs when replacing text meaningfully.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: A product uses instruction fine-tuning, a system prompt, and a policy filter around one LLM. Which alignment map is most useful?
flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: Some alignment changes can be durable training changes, while other controls steer or filter the current run.
flagged_question_repairs.recommended_change: Shorten the correct answer or lengthen/tighten distractors so the correct choice is not obviously more complete.
flagged_question_repairs.implementation_instruction: Codex should revise this question only if the issue is confirmed in source data; preserve stable IDs unless meaning changes, and create new IDs when replacing text meaningfully.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: Why can pretraining create broad capability without making the model a perfect library of its sources?

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: If a system solves a task only by following hand-written if-then rules, how is it different from an LLM?
flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: The rule-based system applies explicit rules; an LLM’s fluency mostly comes from learned weights shaped by examples.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: A student asks a chatbot one question during ordinary use. Which statement is usually true?
flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: The model uses fixed weights and temporary context; the chat usually does not rewrite the weights.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: Why can training change how the model behaves tomorrow, while inference usually cannot?
flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: Training changes parameters that future runs reuse; inference uses temporary computations for the current run.
flagged_question_repairs.recommended_change: Shorten the correct answer or lengthen/tighten distractors so the correct choice is not obviously more complete.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: When an LLM answers a prompt by generating one token at a time, what is the model doing at each step?
flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: It scores possible next tokens from the current context, selects one, appends it, and repeats.
flagged_question_repairs.recommended_change: Shorten the correct answer or lengthen/tighten distractors so the correct choice is not obviously more complete.
flagged_question_repairs.implementation_instruction: Codex should revise this question only if the issue is confirmed in source data; preserve stable IDs unless meaning changes, and create new IDs when replacing text meaningfully.

flagged_question_repairs.current_stem: During ordinary inference, the current context enters the model. What changes temporarily, and what usually stays fixed?

flagged_question_repairs.correct_choice_text: Context enters a forward pass, temporary states form, logits are produced, and sampling can choose a token.

implementation_policy.preserve_ids: Preserve questionId/choiceId when wording is only clarified. Use new stable IDs when a question or option changes meaning.
implementation_policy.correctness: Every question must keep exactly 4 choices and exactly 1 correct answer.
implementation_policy.feedback: Wrong-answer feedback must teach the selected misconception without revealing the correct answer prematurely.
implementation_policy.live_status: Keep full bank active for development only; badge remains under construction pending human review.
implementation_policy.reports: Regenerate a full PDF review packet and JSON/CSV artifacts after changes.
